title: Arnie Meets The Kids

jokes.text: Bleam niki schlev rosko iska bedosko pavitz iba da.
jokes.setup: Someone receives exciting news in a foreign letter
jokes.punchline: The 'wonderful news' is delivered in complete gibberish
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor playing on foreign language tropes, well-integrated with character backstory

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I got letter from my country! Very exciting news!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: What is it? What does it say?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Zizmizzy flibbertig borfenstock, zimsomtible pliddlepoof, mibberdiggin floozenstein!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: That's wonderful news!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I know! I'm so happy!

jokes.text: The stamp commemorates goat cheese
jokes.setup: Looking at foreign letter trying to understand it
jokes.punchline: Of all things to commemorate on a stamp, goat cheese is wonderfully mundane
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail that feels authentically foreign and arbitrary

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Of all things to commemorate on a stamp, goat cheese is wonderfully mundane

jokes.text: They would like me to be the ambassador to France
jokes.setup: All the buildup about a wonderful job opportunity
jokes.punchline: Ambassador to France sounds incredibly prestigious for a taxi dispatcher's wife
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds up to something that sounds impossibly prestigious for these characters

dialogue.speaker: Simka
dialogue.line: Ambassador to France sounds incredibly prestigious for a taxi dispatcher's wife

jokes.text: It pays $2.75 a hour
jokes.setup: Ambassador to France sounds like a prestigious, high-paying position
jokes.punchline: The salary is barely above minimum wage
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of the prestigious job reveal with hilariously low pay

dialogue.speaker: Simka
dialogue.line: Ambassador to France sounds like a prestigious, high-paying position. The salary is barely above minimum wage.

jokes.text: Because I'm the only one who speaks French
jokes.setup: Why would they want Simka as ambassador to France?
jokes.punchline: The entire diplomatic corps of her country apparently doesn't speak French
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic that suggests her entire country is diplomatically incompetent

dialogue.speaker: Simka
dialogue.line: The entire diplomatic corps of her country apparently doesn't speak French

jokes.text: Hey, she really does speak French
jokes.setup: Simka just responded in French
jokes.punchline: Tony's delayed realization states the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat, functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... she's speaking French!

jokes.text: I'm not even going to pester you with questions about whether you had a wonderful time. However, if you'd care to volunteer the information, I'd be glad to hear it
jokes.setup: Claims he won't pester her with questions
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows up asking for the same information
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Arnie's needy but self-aware personality

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: I won't pester you with a lot of questions.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: So what's your name?

jokes.text: decency prevents me from divulging the details, but, you know, it had to do with water beds, baby oil...
jokes.setup: Claims decency prevents him from sharing details
jokes.punchline: Immediately starts sharing salacious details anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Arnie's lack of filter despite good intentions

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: I'd love to tell you all the juicy details, but decency prevents me from sharing them.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: So there was this woman, see, and she had this thing she liked to do...

jokes.text: They know I'm kidding. Yeah, well, I don't think they do. Really? Well, great.
jokes.setup: Arnie thinks his sexual innuendo joke landed well
jokes.punchline: Elaine corrects him and he's delighted they believed it
jokes.explanation: Shows Arnie's social cluelessness and inappropriate pride

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Did you hear the one about the traveling salesman?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: No, what?

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Well, he pulls into this little town and checks into a hotel. The bellhop says, 'Want me to send a girl up to your room?' The salesman says, 'Sure!' Two minutes later, there's a knock on the door. It's the ugliest woman he's ever seen. He says, 'Get outta here!' She says, 'Your loss, pal. I charge fifty bucks and I do things your wife won't do.' He says, 'Like what?' She says, 'I'll leave!'

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: That's awful, Arnie.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Awful? You actually believed there was a woman who wouldn't leave!

jokes.text: but now that you mention it, I must say I went for the gold and I got it!
jokes.setup: Initially says he didn't think of dating as a contest
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with Olympic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Arnie can't help but be boastful

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: I don't think of dating as a contest.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: But if it were, I'd be going for the gold.

jokes.text: You know what? I think it's his boyish insecurity. It's got to be. There's nothing else.
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out why Elaine likes Arnie
jokes.punchline: Tony's baffled conclusion that insecurity must be attractive since there's nothing else
jokes.explanation: Tony's confusion at Elaine's taste in men, delivered with perfect deadpan

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I don't understand it. The guy's got nothing going for him. He's not good-looking, he's not smart, he's not even nice. So what is it? What could she possibly see in him?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You know what it is? It's gotta be the insecurity. That's all that's left. Insecurity must be attractive.

jokes.text: I thought maybe then I could walk you home and we could do it till we pass out
jokes.setup: Louie nervously asking a woman out for soda
jokes.punchline: Escalates from innocent soda to graphic sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - starts sweet then reveals his crude true nature

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Hey beautiful, how about you and me go get a soda sometime?

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: A soda? Sure, that sounds nice.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Maybe dinner too?

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Oh, okay...

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: And then we could go back to my place, get a little more comfortable...

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Well, I don't know...

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Come on, don't be shy. I'm thinking we really get to know each other, if you know what I mean. Real intimate. I'm talking about some serious—

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Goodbye, Louie.

jokes.text: You usually get slapped much harder than that
jokes.setup: Louie just got slapped for his crude proposition
jokes.punchline: Implies this is routine and he usually deserves worse
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing Louie's track record with women

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Louie, you're lucky she only slapped you. Last week a woman threw a drink in your face.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well that was different.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: The week before that, someone hit you with a purse.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, you're getting off easy. You should be grateful.

jokes.text: Are you kidding, Alex? The kids got to love him. He's like Yoda.
jokes.setup: Alex asks what Elaine's kids think of Arnie
jokes.punchline: Tony compares the mild-mannered Arnie to Yoda
jokes.explanation: Funny comparison that captures both Arnie's wisdom and oddness

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: They think he's Yoda.

jokes.text: In an overprotective, paranoid sort of way is what I mean
jokes.setup: Alex agrees that Elaine's reasoning sounds reasonable
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts his agreement with criticism
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup, though somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You're right, that makes sense.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Of course, you're completely wrong about everything else.

jokes.text: You're gonna trust your kids to a guy who promises never to bathe again until Seattle wins the Super Bowl?
jokes.setup: Elaine asking Louie to babysit
jokes.punchline: Alex points out Louie's bizarre hygiene pledge tied to sports
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd character detail that feels authentically Louie

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Louie, would you mind watching my daughter?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I'd be happy to.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Louie, that's very generous of you, but I have to warn you - Louie has this strange habit. He won't bathe until his favorite team wins.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: That's right. And they haven't won in three years.

jokes.text: I pretended to have a heart attack and die
jokes.setup: Louie explaining how he dealt with kids being attached to him
jokes.punchline: His solution was to fake his own death
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and perfectly in character - only Louie would think faking death is a reasonable solution to relationship problems

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: So I faked my own death.

jokes.text: she was profuse in her gratitude
jokes.setup: After describing traumatizing a woman and her kids
jokes.punchline: Louie genuinely thinks she was grateful for the trauma
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the story showing Louie's complete lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: She was grateful! She thanked me!

jokes.text: I'm great with kids
jokes.setup: Just finished describing traumatizing children by faking his death
jokes.punchline: Concludes he's great with kids
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of complete delusion - the irony is devastating

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: I'm great with kids.

jokes.text: Je vais continuer jusqu'à la fin de mes jours
jokes.setup: How long will she keep acting like a child
jokes.punchline: Responds in French with what sounds ominous
jokes.explanation: Continues the French theme with escalating threat

dialogue.speaker: Simka
dialogue.line: Je vais continuer jusqu'à ce que quelqu'un m'arrête.

jokes.text: my French is a little rusty, but if I were you, I'd have someone taste my coffee in the morning
jokes.setup: Translating what Simka said in French
jokes.punchline: Implies she threatened to poison Latka
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the domestic dispute into murder territory

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: She said if you don't come home, she's going to poison you.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: That's nice.

jokes.text: I knew that you would love me enough to do the right thing if I made your life a living hell
jokes.setup: Romantic reunion after Latka gives in
jokes.punchline: She openly admits her strategy was psychological warfare
jokes.explanation: Perfect relationship dynamics - love through manipulation

dialogue.speaker: Simka
dialogue.line: You see, Latka? I tell you my strategy. I use psychological warfare!

dialogue.speaker: Simka
dialogue.line: I make you think you win, but really, I win the whole time!

jokes.text: We broke off diplomatic relations with France
jokes.setup: Just resolved the whole ambassador crisis
jokes.punchline: The job no longer exists anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic resolution making their entire conflict meaningless

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: The job no longer exists anyway

jokes.text: I put out, man!
jokes.setup: Explaining how hard he works to get people to like him
jokes.punchline: Uses crude sexual terminology inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Arnie's social awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: I work hard to get people to like me. I'm friendly, I'm helpful, I'm considerate. But you know what? Sometimes I think I could walk up to somebody and say, 'Hey, let me eat your—' and they'd still think I'm a nice guy.

jokes.text: The key to popularity was knowing how to play dodgeball and how to make those little sounds with your armpits
jokes.setup: Arnie explaining his childhood social failures
jokes.punchline: Reduces childhood popularity to two very specific skills
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures childhood social hierarchies through Arnie's neurotic lens

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: You know, I was never popular in school. I had no social skills, no athletic ability. But I did have two things going for me.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: I could burp the alphabet, and I could make my armpit make noise like a chicken.

jokes.text: Was Buffalo Bob himself? Was Bozo the Clown himself?
jokes.setup: Tony tells him to just be himself around kids
jokes.punchline: Points out that successful children's entertainers are all fake personas
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic that children prefer artificial personas over authenticity

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Be myself? Tony, you ever notice that all the successful children's entertainers are completely fake personas? Bozo, Bugs Bunny, Mr. Rogers—none of them are real people. They're all acts!

jokes.text: Your conjugal visit's over.
jokes.setup: Telling Latka and Simka to leave
jokes.punchline: Comparing their time together to prison visits
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie cynicism with good economy and perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: You two, get outta here! Go on, beat it!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: But Louie, we just got here.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I don't care. Every time you two come around, it's like visiting day at Sing Sing. You depress me.

dialogue.speaker: Simka
dialogue.line: We depress you?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, you sit there, you don't say nothing, and when you leave, I feel like I gotta do another five to ten.

jokes.text: Did Nardo ever mention a guy named Peterson? Elaine adored him. When he met the kids, they hated him.
jokes.setup: Louie asking about Elaine's dating history
jokes.punchline: Sets up ominous precedent for Arnie
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the ghost story that follows

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: So what's the story with you and Arnie?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: What do you mean? There's no story.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Come on, I seen the way he looks at you.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Louie, Arnie's just a friend.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's what they all say. You know what happened to the last guy you said that about?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Neither do I. He disappeared.

jokes.text: when the moon is full, you can hear his voice saying, 'Elaine, I thought we had something special... special... special...'
jokes.setup: Building mysterious legend about Peterson
jokes.punchline: Ghost story ends with pathetic romantic pleading
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of ghost story tropes with romantic desperation

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: So there's this ghost, right? And he's been haunting this place for a hundred years, scaring everybody, making chains rattle, the whole nine yards.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: But then one night, he sees this beautiful woman, and he realizes he's been alone all this time. So he materializes in front of her and says, 'Please... just give me a chance. I know I'm dead, but couldn't you just... couldn't you just go out with me?'

jokes.text: But it's moments like this that make up for it
jokes.setup: Louie admits he sometimes hates himself
jokes.punchline: Tormenting others compensates for self-hatred
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - honest self-awareness paired with cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Sometimes I hate myself.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: That's why I gotta torment others. It's compensation.

jokes.text: Oh, well, I guess I'll have to trade you in for two new kids
jokes.setup: Kid worried Arnie won't like them
jokes.punchline: Mom jokes about trading them in like defective merchandise
jokes.explanation: Standard parental teasing, reasonably well executed

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Arnie's going to love you.

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: What if he doesn't?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Then we'll just have to trade you in. You come with a warranty, don't you?

jokes.text: Can we trade her in?
jokes.setup: Mom just joked about trading them in
jokes.punchline: Kid immediately wants to trade in his sister
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic following up on the joke premise

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: Trade in my sister? Where do I sign?

jokes.text: I'd like you to meet the greatest guy in the world... Arnie Ross!
jokes.setup: Building up Arnie with superlatives
jokes.punchline: The massive buildup for mild-mannered Arnie
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Arnie's overcompensation that follows

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Arnie is the most incredible man I've ever met. He's brilliant, he's sophisticated, he's charming, he's witty, he's athletic...

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: I like ice cream.

jokes.text: I'll bet the girls are just crazy about you, but you don't give them the time of day. Right?
jokes.setup: Arnie trying to connect with young boy
jokes.punchline: Projects adult dating dynamics onto a child
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Arnie's inappropriate assumptions

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Hey kid, you want to go out sometime? Maybe catch a movie?

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: Sure, mister.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Great! But here's the thing... I'm gonna need some space. You know, see other kids on the weekends.

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Nothing personal, but I'm not ready for anything serious right now. We should probably keep this casual.

jokes.text: A mouse or a rat?
jokes.setup: Asking about the kid's favorite animal
jokes.punchline: Offers two very similar and unpopular choices
jokes.explanation: Perfectly shows Arnie's social awkwardness in his limited options

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: What's your favorite animal, kid?

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Well, how about a hyena or a warthog?

jokes.text: Are you kidding? I'm fighting for my life here
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if he wants to take off his coat
jokes.punchline: Arnie treats meeting kids like life-or-death combat
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Arnie's anxiety about meeting children

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Do you want to take off your coat?

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: No, I can't. I'm ready for combat at any moment. You never know when a kid might attack.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: I've got my coat on, my shoes laced tight, and I'm standing near the door. If a child makes one wrong move, I'm out of here.

jokes.text: Aw, give me a break. Then a snake.
jokes.setup: Jennifer says her favorite animal is an octopus
jokes.punchline: When Arnie protests, she picks an even harder animal to make with balloons
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing the kid is smarter than Arnie expected

dialogue.speaker: Jennifer
dialogue.line: My favorite animal is an octopus.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: An octopus? That's impossible to make with balloons!

dialogue.speaker: Jennifer
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. My favorite animal is a porcupine.

jokes.text: Well, then let's all get stinking drunk!
jokes.setup: Elaine says wine won't seem reckless
jokes.punchline: Arnie escalates to suggesting everyone get drunk
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Arnie's poor judgment under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Wine won't seem reckless.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: You're right. Let's get drunk.

jokes.text: What about money?!
jokes.setup: Running out of ideas to impress the kids
jokes.punchline: Resorts to the most obvious bribe
jokes.explanation: Perfect desperation move - when all else fails, try cash

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Hey kids, you want to go to the amusement park?

dialogue.speaker: Kids
dialogue.line: Yeah!

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Or we could go to the movies?

dialogue.speaker: Kids
dialogue.line: Okay...

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Or I could give you money and you can do whatever you want.

dialogue.speaker: Kids
dialogue.line: Money! Yeah!

jokes.text: Let them earn it. In return for the money, they could just come over to my place tomorrow and put in some copper plumbing
jokes.setup: Trying to justify giving kids money
jokes.punchline: Suggests child labor as payment
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd solution that makes the situation worse

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Well, you gotta give kids money. It teaches them the value of a dollar.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: So I say, 'You want five bucks? Go wash my car.' That's not child labor, that's education.

jokes.text: You're ugly! You're stupid! I hate you, Mom!
jokes.setup: Mom making kids return the money
jokes.punchline: Kid completely turns on mom in front of her date
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment but relatively predictable outcome

dialogue.speaker: Mom
dialogue.line: You have to return that money right now.

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: But Mom, I need it!

dialogue.speaker: Mom
dialogue.line: I don't care. Go return it.

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: You're the worst mother ever! You ruin everything! Why do you always embarrass me? Nobody else's mom is this mean!

dialogue.speaker: Mom
dialogue.line: Honey, I can explain...

jokes.text: You have beautiful children, Elaine
jokes.setup: Kids just screamed at their mother
jokes.punchline: Arnie's polite compliment in the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan timing - polite observation during complete chaos

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Well, you certainly have a lovely voice!

jokes.text: Would $40 make you feel better?
jokes.setup: Elaine says she's upset with him
jokes.punchline: Offers her money too, same solution he used on kids
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Arnie learned nothing from the experience

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: What? What did I do? Here, here's some money.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Money? That's your solution to everything?

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Well, it works on my kids.

jokes.text: I should be sterilized
jokes.setup: Arnie lamenting his failure with children
jokes.punchline: Suggests extreme medical intervention
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that's perfectly in character for neurotic Arnie

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: I'm terrible with kids. I can't connect with them at all.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: You know what? Maybe I should just have my brain surgically removed and replaced with a basketball.

jokes.text: Kids... Good night
jokes.setup: Given chance to redeem himself with the children
jokes.punchline: Just says good night instead of trying
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Arnie gives up completely in two words

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Good night.

jokes.text: Just not as much
jokes.setup: Arnie asks if they'd still like him without bribes
jokes.punchline: Kid's brutally honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly devastating honesty delivered matter-of-factly by a child

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Would you still like me if I didn't bring you presents?

dialogue.speaker: Jason
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: They're honest. Yeah. I like them.
jokes.setup: Just got brutally honest feedback from kids
jokes.punchline: Appreciates their honesty despite it being crushing
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Arnie's resilience and self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: You know, I appreciate their honesty. I really do. Even if it is crushing.

jokes.text: Arnie's even cuter than Ricky Schroeder
jokes.setup: Kids expressing love for Arnie after dinner
jokes.punchline: Comparison to 1980s child star
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference that shows kids' affection

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: We love you, Arnie!

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Well, I love you kids too.

dialogue.speaker: Jennifer
dialogue.line: You know, Arnie, you're like a 1980s child star.

dialogue.speaker: Arnie
dialogue.line: Really? Which one?

dialogue.speaker: Jennifer
dialogue.line: I don't know... one that didn't make it.

jokes.text: What do you say I march in there and, uh, kick their butts all over the room?
jokes.setup: Kids now love him too much, creating new problem
jokes.punchline: Suggests violence against children as solution
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly logical solution from Arnie's perspective

jokes.text: I guess I am. You know what? What? I love it.
jokes.setup: Elaine realizes she might be stuck with Arnie
jokes.punchline: She decides she's happy about it
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending that resolves the relationship tension

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... I'm stuck with Arnie.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm happy about it.

jokes.text: I guess I am.
jokes.setup: Arnie saying she may be stuck with him now
jokes.punchline: Elaine's resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective delivery that caps the romantic subplot

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I guess you're right. I'm stuck with you.

tmdb_overview: Elaine and her beau share butterflies over his meeting her children.
insight: Arnie's custody battle spawns 51 jokes in 22 minutes—Season 5's densest episode by volume.
